issues.description: File does not exist. REQ-001/REQ-003/TASK-004 require this file to register the production MCP server with Claude Code (command='node', args=['mcp-server/dist/server.js'], env.KNOWLEDGE_ROOT='.knowledge'). The .claude/ directory exists but contains only a worktrees/ subdirectory. Note: the PRD explicitly acknowledges this file cannot be created by Claude agents due to sandbox restrictions — this is a known infrastructure blocker, not a code defect.
issues.suggestion: Create .claude/mcp_servers.json with content: {"mcpServers":{"knowledge":{"command":"node","args":["mcp-server/dist/server.js"],"env":{"KNOWLEDGE_ROOT":".knowledge"}}}}. Must be done outside the Claude agent sandbox by the project owner or an operator-level script.

issues.description: File does not exist. REQ-002/REQ-003/TASK-004 require this file for the development MCP server configuration (command='npx', args=['tsx','mcp-server/server.ts'], env.KNOWLEDGE_ROOT='.knowledge'). Same sandbox constraint applies as for mcp_servers.json.
issues.suggestion: Create .claude/mcp_servers.dev.json with content: {"mcpServers":{"knowledge":{"command":"npx","args":["tsx","mcp-server/server.ts"],"env":{"KNOWLEDGE_ROOT":".knowledge"}}}}. Must be done outside the Claude agent sandbox.

issues.description: tsconfig.mcp.json uses 'module: NodeNext' inherited from the base tsconfig.json rather than overriding to 'module: CommonJS'. Because package.json has '"type": "module"', TypeScript with NodeNext module mode will produce ESM output, not CommonJS as required by the architecture spec and REQ-004 ('mcp-server/dist/server.js must exist and be a valid CommonJS module'). In practice, Node.js >= 12 runs ESM natively so the server will still start, but the architecture constraint is violated and the compiled artifact is not CJS.
issues.suggestion: Add '"module": "CommonJS"' to tsconfig.mcp.json compilerOptions to produce CommonJS output regardless of the root package.json type:module setting. Also add '"moduleResolution": "Node"' to match CJS resolution semantics and remove the '.js' extensions from relative imports in mcp-server/server.ts (lines 12-17) since CJS does not require them.

issues.description: REQ-014 (priority: should) requires replacing console.error() with process.stderr.write() to match the logging convention used throughout the rest of the codebase. The current code uses console.error('[GetFileSummaryTool] Failed to parse cache.json:', err) which is functionally correct but inconsistent with all other files that use process.stderr.write exclusively.
issues.suggestion: Replace line 29 with: process.stderr.write(`[GetFileSummaryTool] Failed to parse cache.json: ${err}\n`);

issues.description: REQ-015 (priority: should) requires renaming 'scheduleFlushed' to 'scheduleFlush' at its declaration and all call sites. The function is declared as 'scheduleFlushed' on line 64 and called on lines 88, 94, and 100. The passive-voice past-tense name is grammatically incorrect for an action-scheduling function.
issues.suggestion: Rename the function declaration at line 64 from 'function scheduleFlushed()' to 'function scheduleFlush()' and update the three call sites at lines 88, 94, and 100 accordingly.

issues.description: REQ-016 (priority: could) requires a code comment near the ts-morph Project instantiation documenting the known trade-off: skipFileDependencyResolution: true speeds up single-file incremental processing to under 1 second but may leave stale calledBy entries for cross-file call edges. No such comment exists.
issues.suggestion: Add a comment above line 68: // NOTE: skipFileDependencyResolution enables <1s incremental updates but may produce stale calledBy data for cross-file edges — symbols in other files calling changed symbols will not be re-resolved. A full rebuild is needed for accurate cross-file call graphs.

issues.description: REQ-010 requires verifying that actual module names (not 'undefined') appear in get_dependencies output to confirm the TASK-002 bug fix is operational. The current test queries 'sample-project' but all fixture files map to a single module with no cross-module edges, so the BFS yields zero results and the result.to code path is never executed. The fix correctness cannot be end-to-end validated by this fixture.
issues.suggestion: Either add files in subdirectories to the sample-project fixture to create cross-module edges (so result.to is exercised in the integration test), or add a unit test in get-dependencies.test.ts that mocks a DependencyGraph with edges and asserts the formatted output contains actual module names rather than 'undefined'.

issues.description: CallToolResult interface is redefined locally in find-callers.ts, get-dependencies.ts, get-file-summary.ts, and search-architecture.ts rather than imported from a shared location. This duplication risks divergence if the interface changes.
issues.suggestion: Export CallToolResult from mcp-server/types.ts and import it in all tool files to centralise the type definition.

issues.description: The startup log 'process.stderr.write("[mcp-server] Knowledge MCP server started\n")' is written on line 133 after 'await server.connect(transport)' on line 131. If connect() were to block, the startup message would never be written. StdioServerTransport.connect() is non-blocking in practice, but the ordering is misleading.
issues.suggestion: Move the startup log to before the 'await server.connect(transport)' call so it is unconditionally written before any I/O begins.

summary: Core implementation tasks (TASK-001 MCP server entry point, TASK-002 get-dependencies bug fix, TASK-003 package.json scripts, TASK-005 integration test suite) are all completed and the code quality is generally high. The mcp-server/server.ts correctly uses McpServer with StdioServerTransport, routes all 6 tools, reads KNOWLEDGE_ROOT from env, and keeps all logging on stderr. The get-dependencies.ts bug fix (result.to vs result.dependency) is correctly applied. The integration test suite in mcp-tools.test.ts provides thorough coverage with proper beforeAll/afterAll lifecycle management. However, two critical blockers remain: the .claude/mcp_servers.json and .claude/mcp_servers.dev.json config files are absent (acknowledged PRD sandbox constraint, not an agent code defect, but must be resolved before deployment). Additionally, tsconfig.mcp.json does not override the base NodeNext module setting to CommonJS as the architecture explicitly requires, and the two explicitly-scoped should-priority code quality fixes (REQ-014 console.error replacement in get-file-summary.ts, REQ-015 scheduleFlushed rename in watch.ts) were not applied. All of these items should be addressed before the implementation can be considered production-ready.
